title: Logical Access Restrictions
criterion_text: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives. Controls include user identification and authentication, network segmentation, and encryption.
points_of_focus: Identifies and inventories information assets|Restricts logical access to information assets|Identifies and authenticates users prior to granting access|Considers network segmentation to isolate sensitive systems|Manages credentials and authentication infrastructure|Protects encryption keys and other credentials from unauthorized use
source_code_relevance: Look for: authentication middleware, JWT/session validation, RBAC enforcement, MFA checks, network segmentation config, TLS enforcement, environment-based access gating.
testing_approach: Confirm auth middleware on protected routes, RBAC enforced at service layer, MFA conditional logic present, JWT validated for issuer and expiry.

title: User Provisioning and Deprovisioning
criterion_text: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users. For those users whose access is administered by the entity, user system credentials are removed when user access is no longer authorized.
points_of_focus: Controls access credentials based on authorization|Removes access when user is no longer authorized|Reviews appropriateness of access on a periodic basis|Restricts access to register and authorize new users|Manages credentials for external parties including vendors and partners
source_code_relevance: Look for: user registration flows, account creation API, account deactivation/deletion logic, access revocation on offboarding triggers, role assignment at creation time.
testing_approach: Confirm code paths exist for both user creation with role assignment and access revocation/deactivation on termination events.

title: Role-Based Access and Least Privilege
criterion_text: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and related policies, maintaining least privilege and segregation of duties principles.
points_of_focus: Creates and maintains defined access roles for authorized users|Implements role-based access controls restricting access to authorized functions|Maintains segregation of duties preventing single person control over critical processes|Reviews access roles periodically and adjusts as business needs change|Restricts privileged access to authorized users
source_code_relevance: Look for: RBAC implementations, permission checks on routes, role definitions, IaC IAM policies with bounded action sets, admin vs user segregation.
testing_approach: Verify role definitions are narrowly scoped, admin functions are gated, IaC IAM policies avoid wildcard actions like Action: '*'.

title: Physical Access Restrictions
criterion_text: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets (for example, data center equipment, backup media) to authorized personnel to meet the entity's objectives.
points_of_focus: Restricts physical access to facilities and protected assets to authorized individuals|Manages physical access to hardware devices and equipment|Controls visitor access with escort and logging requirements|Maintains physical access logs for sensitive areas
source_code_relevance: Minimal source code signal. Cloud-hosted orgs inherit physical controls from their CSP. May find cloud region/provider configs. Flag for manual reviewer.
testing_approach: For cloud-hosted systems confirm cloud provider is named (physical controls inherited). Flag any self-hosted infra for manual reviewer follow-up.

title: Data Disposal and Asset Decommissioning
criterion_text: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover information from those assets has been diminished and is no longer required to meet the entity's objectives.
points_of_focus: Removes data from assets scheduled for disposal using industry-standard processes|Protects assets slated for disposal until data is no longer recoverable|Documents disposal procedures for physical and logical media|Implements automated deletion schedules aligned to data classification
source_code_relevance: Look for: data deletion/purge jobs, retention policy constants, anonymization logic on user termination, scheduled cleanup cron jobs.
testing_approach: Look for scheduled data purge jobs, user account deletion logic, anonymization functions tied to retention policy constants.

title: Boundary Protection Against External Threats
criterion_text: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries, including identifying and managing vulnerabilities, monitoring unauthorized access attempts, and enforcing additional authentication for external access.
points_of_focus: Restricts access to information assets from external sources using firewalls or other boundary protection|Protects identification and authentication credentials from external threats|Monitors system boundaries for unauthorized access attempts|Enforces additional authentication requirements for external access|Manages boundary protection of subservice organizations
source_code_relevance: Look for: CORS policy config, rate limiting middleware, API gateway and WAF configs, network security group ingress rules in IaC, TLS termination, HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect.
testing_approach: Confirm CORS origins are explicitly allowlisted, rate limiting is on public endpoints, network ingress rules restrict open ports, HTTP redirects to HTTPS.

title: Secure Data Transmission and Movement
criterion_text: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity's objectives.
points_of_focus: Restricts the ability to perform data transmission to authorized users|Encrypts personal and sensitive data during transmission using TLS 1.2 or higher|Protects mobile devices used to transmit and store sensitive data|Prevents unauthorized removal of information from the system boundaries|Enforces secure cookie attributes on session tokens
source_code_relevance: Look for: TLS version enforcement, HTTPS-only configs, SSL on DB connections, cookie security flags (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite), secret scanning for exposed keys in transit.
testing_approach: Verify TLS minimum version in HTTP client configs, DB connections require SSL, cookies set with HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite, no plaintext secrets in code.

title: Prevention of Unauthorized or Malicious Software
criterion_text: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software to meet the entity's objectives.
points_of_focus: Detects unauthorized or malicious software through scanning procedures|Monitors system components for the introduction of unauthorized software|Implements allowlisting or other controls to prevent execution of unauthorized software|Scans third-party software and dependencies for known vulnerabilities before use|Protects detection tools from unauthorized access or disabling
source_code_relevance: Look for: SAST tools in CI/CD YAML, SCA/dependency scanning steps, lock files committed, Dockerfile base image pinning, artifact integrity checks, pre-commit hooks.
testing_approach: Confirm SAST step in CI pipeline YAML, SCA scan runs before deploy, base images are pinned not 'latest', lock files are committed to repo.

title: Vulnerability Detection and Management
criterion_text: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify (1) changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities, and (2) susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.
points_of_focus: Uses a defined vulnerability management program to identify and remediate vulnerabilities|Monitors industry sources for new vulnerabilities and threats|Performs vulnerability scanning on system components|Implements penetration testing to identify and assess vulnerabilities|Prioritizes vulnerabilities based on risk for timely remediation
source_code_relevance: Look for: Dependabot or Renovate config, scheduled security scan configs, CVE scan integrations in CI/CD, pinned dependency versions, CVSS threshold gates.
testing_approach: Confirm automated dependency update or vulnerability scan config exists, CI pipeline fails on high/critical CVEs, scan results are captured as build artifacts.

title: Security Event Monitoring and Alerting
criterion_text: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives, and generates alerts when such anomalies are detected.
points_of_focus: Implements detection policies that define the frequency and method of monitoring|Monitors infrastructure and software for anomalous behavior|Monitors for unauthorized access attempts and unusual access patterns|Implements threshold-based alerts for security-relevant events|Generates audit records including system components, user identification, and actions taken
source_code_relevance: Look for: structured audit logging on auth events, centralized log forwarding config, alerting definitions in IaC, failed login attempt logging, security event triggers.
testing_approach: Verify audit log calls on auth events (login, logout, failed attempts, privilege changes), logs output structured JSON, log forwarding config points to centralized sink.

title: Security Incident Evaluation and Classification
criterion_text: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures.
points_of_focus: Evaluates security events using defined classification criteria|Identifies whether a security event constitutes a security incident|Applies a defined incident classification tier to prioritize response|Communicates identified incidents to appropriate parties|Captures sufficient context in event records to support incident classification
source_code_relevance: Look for: incident classification logic, severity tier constants, escalation code in exception handlers, PagerDuty/Opsgenie integration, error severity mapping.
testing_approach: Confirm error handlers classify security failures distinctly, critical errors trigger external alerting integration, severity tier constants are defined in code.

title: Incident Response Procedures
criterion_text: The entity responds to identified security incidents by: executing an incident response program; communicating with affected parties; performing root cause analysis; and implementing remediation to prevent future incidents.
points_of_focus: Assigns roles and responsibilities for incident response|Communicates incident status and response actions to affected parties|Executes defined incident response procedures including containment and eradication|Performs root cause analysis after incident resolution|Implements changes to prevent recurrence of security incidents
source_code_relevance: Low signal in raw source code. May find incident runbook docs, notification webhook code, post-mortem templates, or automated remediation scripts in repo.
testing_approach: Check for incident runbook documentation in repo, webhook/notification code for security events, automated remediation scripts.

title: Incident Recovery and Post-Incident Activity
criterion_text: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents and improve the information security program to prevent future incidents.
points_of_focus: Executes defined recovery procedures to restore systems to normal operation|Tests and validates recovery procedures periodically|Communicates post-incident lessons learned to improve controls|Implements improvements identified through post-incident review|Maintains backup and restoration capabilities to support recovery
source_code_relevance: Look for: backup and recovery configs, health check endpoints, database backup schedules in IaC, restore scripts, availability probe configs, DR runbooks.
testing_approach: Confirm health check endpoint exists, backup retention config is present in IaC, restore procedure documented or scripted in repo.

title: Authorized and Tested System Changes
criterion_text: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its change management objectives.
points_of_focus: Manages changes through a defined change management process|Authorizes changes before implementation including patches and updates|Tests system changes prior to deployment using defined testing procedures|Tracks and approves infrastructure-as-code changes through version control|Deploys to production only through authorized automated pipelines|Prevents unauthorized access to program source code
source_code_relevance: Look for: branch protection requiring PR approvals, CI/CD pipeline definitions with required test and scan gates, environment promotion requiring approval, version tagging, code owner rules.
testing_approach: Confirm branch protection enforces >= 1 reviewer, CI workflow has test step that must pass before deploy, production environment has manual approval gate.

title: Business Disruption Risk Mitigation
criterion_text: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.
points_of_focus: Considers options for risk mitigation for risks arising from business disruption|Implements controls to reduce the impact of business disruption events|Maintains and tests contingency plans to recover from disruptions|Implements resilience patterns including redundancy and failover|Monitors availability metrics against defined thresholds
source_code_relevance: Look for: circuit breaker patterns, retry logic with backoff, graceful shutdown handlers, fallback service configs, autoscaling and multi-AZ in IaC.
testing_approach: Check for retry/backoff on external calls, graceful shutdown signal handling, autoscaling config with min > 1, multi-AZ or redundancy flags in IaC.

title: Vendor and Third-Party Risk Management
criterion_text: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners. The entity establishes requirements for third parties and monitors their compliance with those requirements.
points_of_focus: Establishes requirements for vendor and business partner security|Assesses vendor compliance with entity security requirements before engagement|Inventories third-party software components and their known vulnerabilities|Pins and locks third-party dependency versions to prevent supply chain attacks|Monitors for known-malicious or deprecated packages in dependency trees
source_code_relevance: Look for: dependency manifest files (package.json, requirements.txt), pinned versions vs floating ranges, lock files committed to repo, third-party API call patterns, env-injected vendor credentials.
testing_approach: Confirm lock files committed, versions are pinned or bounded, third-party credentials are env vars not hardcoded, no obvious deprecated packages.

title: Capacity and Performance Management
criterion_text: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components to manage capacity demand and to enable the implementation of additional capacity to help meet its objectives.
points_of_focus: Measures current usage levels of system capacity to identify deviations from expected levels|Forecasts capacity needs to support future demand|Implements plans to increase capacity when demand approaches thresholds|Monitors performance and capacity metrics against defined baselines|Notifies relevant parties when capacity thresholds are approaching
source_code_relevance: Look for: autoscaling configs, resource quota definitions, load balancer configs, health check and readiness probe definitions, performance threshold alerts in IaC.
testing_approach: Confirm autoscaling config defines min/max replicas and CPU/memory thresholds, load balancer present, health probes configured for readiness and liveness.

title: Environmental and Infrastructure Protections
criterion_text: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires environmental protection controls to meet availability commitments and system requirements.
points_of_focus: Implements controls to protect against environmental threats including power loss and hardware failure|Deploys systems across multiple availability zones or regions for redundancy|Implements CDN or edge caching to improve availability under load|Maintains secondary infrastructure capable of assuming primary workload|Monitors environmental controls and infrastructure health continuously
source_code_relevance: Look for: multi-region or multi-AZ deployment configs, database replica configs, CDN configs, redundant service definitions in IaC.
testing_approach: Confirm database has multi-AZ or replica config, deployment spans multiple AZs, CDN or failover routing configured in IaC.

title: Backup, Restore, and Recovery Testing
criterion_text: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its availability commitments and system requirements.
points_of_focus: Implements automated backup procedures for critical data and system configurations|Tests the completeness and integrity of backup data periodically|Documents and tests restoration procedures for all critical systems|Defines and tracks recovery time objectives and recovery point objectives|Maintains backup retention periods aligned to business recovery requirements
source_code_relevance: Look for: backup retention configs in IaC (RDS, DynamoDB, Supabase), restore scripts, backup schedule definitions, PITR enablement, deletion protection.
testing_approach: Confirm backup retention >= 7 days, PITR enabled on primary DB, deletion protection active in IaC.

title: Complete and Accurate Processing
criterion_text: The entity obtains or generates, uses, and communicates relevant, quality information regarding the objectives related to processing, including definitions of data processed and product and service specifications.
points_of_focus: Defines and documents processing inputs, outputs, and data schemas|Validates incoming API payloads against predefined schemas|Enforces type constraints and format requirements on all data inputs|Communicates processing errors clearly to upstream systems|Monitors processing completeness against defined success metrics
source_code_relevance: Look for: input schema validation (Pydantic, Joi, Zod, JSON Schema), request payload validation middleware, type enforcement at API boundaries.
testing_approach: Confirm all API endpoints have schema validation on incoming payloads, type errors return 422/400 with structured error, validation is not bypassed in tests.

title: System Processing Commitments and Requirements
criterion_text: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs, including those provided by customers and other external parties, to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives.
points_of_focus: Implements atomicity controls to ensure multi-step operations complete fully or not at all|Uses database transactions to prevent partial data updates|Implements idempotency keys to prevent duplicate processing of critical operations|Detects and handles race conditions in concurrent data access|Logs all state transitions for critical processing operations
source_code_relevance: Look for: database transaction logic, atomicity patterns (try/commit/rollback), idempotency key implementations, duplicate detection, optimistic locking.
testing_approach: Verify multi-step data operations use transactions, idempotency keys are checked before processing critical operations, rollback logic is present.

title: Input Completeness and Accuracy Controls
criterion_text: The entity implements controls over the completeness and accuracy of inputs to processing. Controls include data sanitization, injection prevention, and error handling for malformed inputs.
points_of_focus: Validates completeness and accuracy of all processing inputs before use|Implements parameterized queries or ORM to prevent SQL injection|Sanitizes user-provided content before rendering to prevent XSS|Enforces input length and format constraints to prevent buffer and logic errors|Verifies file integrity using checksums for uploaded or transferred documents
source_code_relevance: Look for: SQL injection prevention (parameterized queries/ORM), XSS sanitization, input length/format constraints, file upload validation and checksum verification.
testing_approach: Confirm ORM or parameterized queries for all DB calls (no raw string concat), XSS sanitization on user HTML content, file uploads validated for type and size.

title: Output Processing and Error Disclosure Controls
criterion_text: The entity implements policies and procedures to make available or deliver output completely, accurately, and timely in accordance with specifications to meet the entity's objectives. Controls include error masking to prevent internal information disclosure.
points_of_focus: Delivers processing outputs that are complete, accurate, and timely|Masks or sanitizes error messages to prevent internal stack trace disclosure|Logs detailed errors internally while returning generic messages externally|Implements global error handling to catch and safely respond to unhandled exceptions|Monitors for unexpected output failures that may indicate processing errors
source_code_relevance: Look for: global error handler masking stack traces in API responses, generic 500 error message patterns, internal error logging distinct from external error responses.
evidence_indicators: catch(err){ return 'Internal Server Error'|app.use((err, req, res|statusCode: 500|message: 'Something went wrong'|sanitize_error|logger.error(err.stack)|global_exception
testing_approach: Confirm global error handler is registered, 500 responses return generic messages not stack traces, stack trace is logged internally but not returned to client.

title: Storage and Transmission Integrity Controls
criterion_text: The entity implements policies and procedures to store and maintain information to meet the entity's objectives. Controls include checksums, hashing for stored sensitive values, and encryption at rest for protected fields.
points_of_focus: Stores sensitive credentials using cryptographically strong hashing algorithms (Argon2, bcrypt)|Encrypts sensitive PII fields at the database or application layer|Enables at-rest encryption on all data storage services (databases, object storage)|Manages encryption keys through a dedicated key management service|Verifies integrity of stored data using checksums or hash comparisons
source_code_relevance: Look for: password hashing (bcrypt, Argon2, PBKDF2), field-level encryption for PII, at-rest encryption configs in IaC (storage_encrypted, SSE), KMS key management.
testing_approach: Confirm password fields use bcrypt/Argon2 not MD5/SHA1, sensitive PII is encrypted before storage, IaC enables at-rest encryption on DB and object storage.

title: Data Minimization
criterion_text: Personal data shall be adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed ('data minimisation'). Controllers must define and enforce minimum necessary data collection at the code level.
points_of_focus: Collects only personal data fields necessary for the stated processing purpose|Avoids collecting optional fields that are not used in processing logic|Implements field-level access controls so internal services receive only necessary data|Documents the purpose for each personal data field collected|Removes or anonymizes personal data fields no longer needed for the original purpose
source_code_relevance: Look for: data models/schemas that collect only necessary PII fields, API request/response objects that trim unnecessary personal data, data projection patterns (select specific columns), purpose-annotated field definitions.
testing_approach: Verify data model collects only stated necessary fields, API responses project minimal PII, no undocumented PII collection visible in schema or model definitions.

title: Storage Limitation
criterion_text: Personal data shall be kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed ('storage limitation'). Controllers must implement and enforce retention periods.
points_of_focus: Defines retention periods for each category of personal data|Implements automated deletion or anonymization of data when retention period expires|Maintains records of data categories and their associated retention schedules|Regularly reviews stored data to confirm continued necessity|Ensures backup and archive systems also respect retention periods
source_code_relevance: Look for: retention period constants, scheduled deletion/anonymization jobs, created_at + TTL patterns, soft-delete with expiry, data purge cron jobs.
testing_approach: Confirm retention period constants are defined in code, automated deletion or anonymization jobs exist, expiry/TTL fields are present on personal data tables.

title: Integrity and Confidentiality of Personal Data
criterion_text: Personal data shall be processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures ('integrity and confidentiality').
points_of_focus: Implements access controls restricting personal data to authorized processors only|Encrypts personal data at rest and in transit|Logs all access and modifications to personal data for auditability|Implements input validation to prevent corruption of personal data|Protects personal data processing systems against unauthorized modification
source_code_relevance: Look for: access controls on PII data endpoints, encryption of PII fields in storage, audit logging on personal data reads/writes, input validation on personal data fields.
testing_approach: Verify PII endpoints are gated behind auth and role checks, PII fields are encrypted at rest, access to personal data is audit-logged with user identity.

title: Data Protection by Design
criterion_text: Taking into account the state of the art and costs of implementation, the controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures designed to implement data-protection principles (such as data minimisation) in an effective manner and to integrate the necessary safeguards into the processing.
points_of_focus: Integrates data protection controls into system architecture from the design stage, not as a retrofit|Implements pseudonymisation or tokenization of personal identifiers where functionally possible|Applies data minimization in data model design, collecting only necessary fields|Designs APIs to return minimal personal data appropriate to the consumer's role|Implements privacy-preserving patterns (separate PII stores, tokenization) in data architecture
source_code_relevance: Look for: pseudonymization patterns, tokenization of PII, separation of identity store from behavioural data, PII-stripped API response shaping, privacy-annotated data models.
testing_approach: Confirm pseudonymization or tokenization is applied to personal identifiers, PII is not unnecessarily propagated through system layers, API responses minimize personal data returned.

title: Data Protection by Default
criterion_text: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for ensuring that, by default, only personal data which are necessary for each specific purpose of the processing are processed. This applies to the amount of data collected, extent of processing, period of storage, and accessibility.
points_of_focus: Configures system defaults to collect minimum personal data rather than requiring users to opt out|Disables optional personal data fields and analytics by default unless user actively consents|Restricts data accessibility to only the internal services that require it for their function|Implements consent-gated data collection where processing relies on consent as a legal basis|Defaults data sharing and third-party integrations to disabled unless explicitly enabled
source_code_relevance: Look for: opt-in vs opt-out defaults in feature flags, consent checks before analytics/tracking code, default privacy settings, minimal-by-default API field exposure.
testing_approach: Confirm analytics/tracking defaults to disabled, consent is checked before any personal data collection, feature flags for optional data collection default to off.

title: Pseudonymisation and Encryption of Personal Data
criterion_text: The controller and processor shall implement appropriate technical measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including the pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data.
points_of_focus: Encrypts personal data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent strong encryption|Encrypts personal data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher|Applies pseudonymisation to personal identifiers where full identification is not required for processing|Manages encryption keys separately from the encrypted data|Implements database-level or field-level encryption for sensitive personal data columns
source_code_relevance: Look for: field-level encryption on PII columns, encryption at rest in IaC (storage_encrypted, SSE), TLS enforcement configs, password hashing with bcrypt/Argon2, pseudonymization of identifiers.
testing_approach: Verify PII fields encrypted at rest in IaC, TLS enforced on all connections, passwords hashed with strong algorithm, no plaintext PII in database column definitions.

title: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability and Resilience
criterion_text: The controller and processor shall implement measures to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services handling personal data.
points_of_focus: Implements access controls ensuring only authorized users can read or modify personal data|Maintains audit logs of all access and modifications to personal data processing systems|Implements redundancy and failover to ensure continued availability of personal data systems|Tests system resilience periodically to confirm recovery objectives are achievable|Monitors personal data processing systems for availability and integrity anomalies
source_code_relevance: Look for: access controls on PII APIs, audit logging on data access, multi-AZ or replica configs for databases holding PII, health check endpoints, integrity check patterns.
testing_approach: Confirm audit logging on all PII data access paths, redundancy config (multi-AZ/replica) on database holding PII, health check endpoint reflects system integrity.

title: Regular Testing and Evaluation of Security Measures
criterion_text: The controller and processor shall implement a process for regularly testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the processing.
points_of_focus: Implements automated security scanning of personal data processing systems in CI/CD pipelines|Performs regular penetration testing targeting systems that process personal data|Maintains a schedule for reviewing and updating security measures|Tracks and remedies security vulnerabilities identified through testing|Documents testing results as evidence of ongoing security evaluation
source_code_relevance: Look for: SAST/DAST in CI/CD pipeline, dependency vulnerability scanning, automated security test suite, scheduled security review configs.
testing_approach: Confirm security scanning tools are present in CI/CD pipeline YAML, scans run on every PR or on a schedule, scan failures block deployment.

title: Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten)
criterion_text: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay where one of the defined grounds applies. The controller shall erase personal data without undue delay.
points_of_focus: Implements an erasure request endpoint or workflow in the application|Deletes or anonymizes all personal data associated with the data subject across all systems|Propagates erasure requests to downstream services and sub-processors|Logs erasure requests and confirms completion with timestamps|Handles erasure of personal data in backup and archive systems
source_code_relevance: Look for: account deletion endpoint, cascading delete logic across related tables, anonymization on deletion, erasure request logging, downstream service notification on erasure.
testing_approach: Confirm a deletion/erasure endpoint exists, cascading deletes cover all related personal data tables, erasure is logged with timestamp, downstream services are notified.

title: Right to Data Portability
criterion_text: The data subject shall have the right to receive the personal data concerning him or her, which he or she has provided to a controller, in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format and have the right to transmit those data to another controller.
points_of_focus: Implements a data export endpoint that returns personal data in structured machine-readable format (JSON, CSV)|Includes all personal data provided by the data subject in the export|Authenticates and authorizes the portability request to the correct data subject|Delivers the export securely (encrypted download or authenticated endpoint)|Logs portability request fulfillment with timestamps
source_code_relevance: Look for: data export endpoint, user data download functionality, export to JSON/CSV, authenticated export generation, personal data packaging logic.
testing_approach: Confirm a data export endpoint exists and returns structured personal data, endpoint requires authentication as the data subject, export covers all personal data fields.

title: Notification of Personal Data Breach to Supervisory Authority
criterion_text: In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority.
points_of_focus: Implements detection mechanisms that identify potential personal data breaches|Defines a breach notification workflow with a 72-hour response target|Logs breach detection events with timestamp of discovery|Captures the categories and approximate number of data subjects affected|Implements automated alerting when unauthorized access to personal data is detected
source_code_relevance: Look for: breach detection alerts, security event monitoring on PII data access, notification webhook or integration for supervisory authority reporting, breach log data structures.
testing_approach: Check for breach detection alerting on unauthorized PII access events, breach notification workflow or runbook in repo, breach log data structure capturing required GDPR fields.

title: PII Masking in Logs and Error Outputs
criterion_text: As part of implementing appropriate technical security measures under Article 32, controllers must ensure personal data is not inadvertently exposed through application logs, error messages, or debugging outputs in violation of the integrity and confidentiality principle.
points_of_focus: Implements log masking or redaction for personal data fields before writing to log sinks|Strips or hashes email addresses, phone numbers, and government IDs from log entries|Prevents personal data from appearing in stack traces or error responses returned to clients|Validates that structured logging formatters exclude PII from all log levels|Audits log infrastructure to confirm personal data cannot be recovered from log storage
source_code_relevance: Look for: PII masking utilities in logging middleware, redaction functions applied before log.write, custom log formatters that strip PII fields, error handler sanitization.
testing_approach: Confirm logging middleware or formatter applies PII masking, email/phone/ID fields are redacted or hashed in log output, error responses do not contain personal data.

title: Input Validation and Injection Prevention for Personal Data
criterion_text: As part of securing personal data processing under Article 32, controllers must protect personal data processing systems against injection attacks and malformed input that could compromise personal data integrity or enable unauthorized disclosure.
points_of_focus: Validates and sanitizes all personal data inputs before processing or storage|Uses parameterized queries or ORM to prevent SQL injection from exposing personal data|Applies XSS sanitization to prevent personal data from being exfiltrated via script injection|Enforces strict content-type validation on personal data upload endpoints|Implements rate limiting on personal data access endpoints to prevent enumeration attacks
source_code_relevance: Look for: parameterized queries/ORM usage on PII tables, XSS sanitization for PII rendered in UI, rate limiting on auth and personal data endpoints, input length constraints on PII fields.
testing_approach: Verify ORM or parameterized queries on all queries touching PII tables, XSS sanitization on PII rendered in HTML, rate limiting on login and PII-returning endpoints.
